solutions.solution:
Statements: I. Some books are clips. II. No rock is a book.
Conclusions: I. No lock is a spoon. II. No spoon is a lock.

Statements: All locks are keys. No key is a spoon.
Conclusions: I. No lock is a spoon. II. No spoon is a lock.

Statements: All young scientists are open-minded. No open-minded men are superstitious.
Conclusions: I. No scientist is superstitious. II. No young people are superstitious.

Statements: Some hats are pins. All pins are watches.
Conclusions: I. No watch is a hat. II. All pins are hats.

Statements: All politicians are intelligent. Some women are politicians.
Conclusions: I. Some women are intelligent. II. All those who are intelligent are women.

Statements: All bulbs are rods. No rod is a gun.
Conclusions: I. All rods are bulbs. II. No bulb is a gun.

Statements: Some ministers are clerks. All clerks are businessmen.
Conclusions: I. Some ministers are businessmen. II. All businessmen are clerks.

Statements: Some desks are caps. No cap is red.
Conclusions: I. Some caps are desks. II. No desk is red.

Statements: All lights are trucks. Some trucks are jeeps.
Conclusions: I. All jeeps are lights. II. Some lights are jeeps.

Statements: Some trees are glasses. All trees are tables.
Conclusions: I. Some trees are glasses. II. Some glasses are trees.

Statements: Most clocks are fans. Some fans are walls.
Conclusions: I. Some walls are fans. II. Some clocks are walls.

Statements: All cars are tables. Some children are tables.
Conclusions: I. Some children are cars. II. All horses are bullocks.

Statements: All bullocks are goats. All horses are goats.
Conclusions: I. All goats are horses. II. Some books are pencils.

Statements: Some pencils are pens. All books are pens.
Conclusions: I. Some pens are books. II. All trays are erasers.

Statements: All pens are erasers. No erasers are pens.
Conclusions: I. Some pens are trays. II. Some pastries are toffees.

Statements: All toffees are chocolates.
Conclusions: I. Some chocolates are toffees. II. Some toffees are not pastries.

Statements: Some chalks are wires. Some wires are frames.
Conclusions: I. Some frames are chalks. II. All scientists are researchers.

Statements: Some researchers are teachers.
Conclusions: I. No scientist is a teacher. II. Some teachers are scientists.

Statements: Some authors are teachers. No teacher is a lady.
Conclusions: I. Some teachers are not ladies. II. Some ladies are teachers.

Statements: No women can vote. Some women are politicians.
Conclusions: I. Male politicians can vote. II. Some politicians can vote.

Statements: All young girls are modern. No modern men are superstitious.
Conclusions: I. No girl is superstitious. II. No young man is superstitious.
